data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== DSO re-explore: coherence-walk + plan-review (lossless + reconcile)
=== agent a0b0aae8f7e033fd2

I now have everything. Both target files and the catalog Part 4 are fully read. Let me verify exact line numbers for a few key citations to ensure precision in my reconciliation, then produce the report.

The catalog cites several specific line ranges. Let me confirm the verdict-rubric discipline lines and the plan-review SKILL/dispatch line numbers match what I read. I have all the content with line numbers from the Reads. Let me compile the exhaustive findings.

---

# LOSSLESS EXTRACTION — DSO coherence-walk + plan-review surfaces

Root: `/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/`. All files read fully. Reconciliation against catalog Part 4 follows each section.

---

## SECTION 1 — Coherence-walk SHARED VERDICT RUBRIC

File: `skills/coherence-walk/prompts/verdict-rubric.md` (135 lines). Prepended to every chunk A–F by the orchestrator.

### 1.1 — The 3-valued PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL system

**name:** Three-verdict semantics
**what:** Each chunk emits exactly one of three verdicts about whether a workflow surface correctly handles the SC-vs-Closure-Checks distinction.
**literal quote (header):** `"Each chunk emits exactly one of three verdicts:"` — **verdict-rubric.md:19**

**PASS** — verdict-rubric.md:21-27
> "The workflow surface handles the SC-vs-Closure-Checks distinction correctly. You have positive evidence (specific file:line citations) that the validation property holds. There may be minor findings (cosmetic, low-stakes documentation gaps) but no important or critical finding."
> A PASS chunk has: `status: "PASS"`; "At least one citation in `evidence_citations` showing where the workflow enforces the distinction"; "Zero critical and zero important findings (may have minor findings)"

**AMBIGUOUS** — verdict-rubric.md:29-39
> "You cannot conclusively determine whether the workflow handles the distinction."
> Causes (literal): "The relevant code path exists but is missing concrete examples or canonical-litmus-test references that would make the behavior verifiable"; "The workflow has dual code paths and you cannot determine which one runs in practice"; "File contents are partially out-of-date with the new schema (some sections updated, others not)"; "The validation property is conceptually unclear in the workflow file itself"
> An AMBIGUOUS chunk has: `status: "AMBIGUOUS"`; "Findings explaining what is unclear and what would resolve the ambiguity"; "May have zero, one, or many citations"

**FAIL** — verdict-rubric.md:41-48
> "The workflow surface conflates SC and Closure Checks in at least one identifiable place, or has a code path that would produce a wrong outcome under the new schema. You can name the specific file:line where the conflation lives."
> A FAIL chunk has: `status: "FAIL"`; "At least one citation pointing to the conflation site"; "At least one finding with severity `critical` or `important` describing the conflation"

**where used:** all six chunks (A–F) reference it: "The rubric defines verdict semantics, finding severity, output format, and cite-or-omit discipline. Follow it." Chunk F adds a chunk-local **verdict mapping** (see 2.6).
**scoring/verdict:** enum {PASS, AMBIGUOUS, FAIL}.
**overlap:** Maps to our block/advise split (catalog SYNTHESIS NOTE 1: FAIL→block, AMBIGUOUS→advisory, PASS→clear). Distinct from plan-review's 2-valued PASS/REVISE (Section 3).

### 1.2 — Severity definitions (critical / important / minor)

**name:** Finding severity tiers — verdict-rubric.md:49-53
- **critical** — verdict-rubric.md:51:
  > "The workflow will produce a wrong outcome at runtime under the new schema (e.g., the completion verifier will pass an epic that has unresolved Closure Checks; brainstorm will refuse a valid end-state SC)."
- **important** — verdict-rubric.md:52:
  > "The workflow has a real gap that will surface as a bug when exercised, but not necessarily a wrong outcome on every run (e.g., refusal copy doesn't cite the canonical litmus test, so users will not know why the rejection happened)."
- **minor** — verdict-rubric.md:53:
  > "Documentation drift, cosmetic gap, missing optional reference. The workflow runs correctly but the artifact could be clearer."

**scoring/verdict:** severity enum {critical, important, minor}; FAIL requires ≥1 critical-or-important; PASS allows only minor.
**overlap:** Severity vocabulary {critical|major|minor} of plan-review (Section 3.6) is similar but uses **major** not **important** — a real lexical divergence between the two surfaces. The catalog tied severity to runtime impact (SYNTHESIS NOTE 4).

### 1.3 — Cross-cutting reviewer DISCIPLINES (the anti-false-positive machinery) — CENTRAL

**(a) Single-question scoping** — verdict-rubric.md:7-16 (header "What you are NOT answering" at :12)
Literal quotes:
> ":9 — "Each chunk asks one question of a different planning-workflow surface: **"does this workflow correctly handle the SC-vs-Closure-Checks distinction now that the epic has shipped its template, validator hook, and verifier refactor?"** Your job is to read the workflow files cited in your chunk prompt and produce a verdict."
> :13 — "You are not reviewing code quality. You are not evaluating implementation correctness. You are not running tests. You are not asking whether the planning artifacts are well-designed in general. You are answering only the narrow SC-vs-Closure-Checks distinction question your chunk prompt names."
> :15 — "If you find unrelated issues, do not include them. Findings outside the chunk's validation property are out of scope."

**(b) Cite-or-omit** — verdict-rubric.md:61-63 (header "## Discipline: cite or omit" at :61)
Literal quote:
> "If you cannot cite a specific file:line for a claim, do NOT make the claim. Findings without evidence are change-detector noise — they create false positives that block legitimate migrations. When in doubt, prefer AMBIGUOUS with a specific question over FAIL with a hand-waved claim."

**(c) Refuse-to-invent / no-speculation** — verdict-rubric.md:65-67 (header "## Refuse-to-invent" at :65)
Literal quote:
> "You read static files. You do NOT speculate about what the workflow "probably" does, "should" do, or "would" do in a hypothetical. Every claim must be grounded in text you actually read. If a file does not exist or is empty, treat that as evidence (cite the absence) — do not infer."

**where used:** all six chunks; the disciplines are the named anti-FP levers ("prefer AMBIGUOUS+question over FAIL+hand-wave").
**scoring/verdict:** Not scored — these are gating disciplines on whether a finding may be emitted at all.
**overlap:** Directly equivalent to plan-review-dispatch.md:2 ("find real problems — not to nitpick or add unnecessary suggestions"), but coherence-walk's is far more explicit/structured. Catalog SYNTHESIS NOTE 2 names these as the direct lever for "Sonnet can answer reliably without false positives."

### 1.4 — Supporting rubric scaffolding (NOT in catalog Part 4 — see reconciliation)

- **"The question you are answering"** (the universal narrow question) — verdict-rubric.md:7-9.
- **"What you are NOT answering"** explicit exclusion list — verdict-rubric.md:11-16.
- **Output format / JSON-only contract** — verdict-rubric.md:55-59:
  > ":57 — "Your output is a single JSON object conforming to `docs/contracts/coherence-walkthrough-chunk-output.md` §1. Required fields: `workflow_stage`, `status`, `evidence_citations`, `findings`. Optional: `tech_failure` (always omit or set to `false` — only the orchestrator sets `tech_failure: true`)."
  > :59 — "Output MUST be the JSON object alone, no surrounding prose, no markdown fence, no explanatory text. The orchestrator parses your output as raw JSON."
- **Orchestrator/context framing** — verdict-rubric.md:1-5: six opus-tier reviewers dispatched by `scripts/coherence-walkthrough.sh` to gate Phase 4 of epic `a03c-d55e-1393-4f27` (Closure Checks schema migration); output aggregated into a report attached to the gated epic.
- **Three worked examples** — verdict-rubric.md:69-130: Example 1 clean PASS (brainstorm, :71-86); Example 2 important→FAIL (sprint, completion-verifier relates_to walk, :88-108, severity `critical`); Example 3 ambiguity→AMBIGUOUS (preplanning Closure Checks partition, :110-129, severity `important`). These encode the verdict+severity+citation contract by demonstration.
- **`tech_failure` field** — orchestrator-only; reviewer always omits/false (:57).

---

## SECTION 2 — Chunk A–F validation properties (every sub-clause)

Each chunk's property is a **conjunction: PASS requires ALL sub-clauses hold** ("if and only if ALL of the following hold").

### 2.1 — Chunk A: brainstorm — chunk-a-brainstorm.md
**Files to read:** `:11-18` — brainstorm SKILL.md; phases/epic-description-template.md, convert-to-epic.md, enrich-in-place.md, approval-gate.md, post-scrutiny-handlers.md; `brainstorm/prompts/*`; `shared/prompts/verifiable-sc-check.md`.
**Validation property (`:22` "if and only if ALL of the following hold"):**
1. **Template separation** — :24: "`phases/epic-description-template.md` contains a `## Closure Checks` section that is structurally distinct from `## Success Criteria`. The template must instruct authors how to choose between the two."
2. **End-state-only rule enforced** — :26: "`shared/prompts/verifiable-sc-check.md` contains the canonical litmus test (\"Could this item be false before the sprint began and true only because of this sprint's specific work? If yes, route to Closure Checks.\") with at least one accept example AND at least one reject example."
3. **Refusal copy present** — :28: "The brainstorm dialogue produces a rejection message when an author submits a transitional SC, naming the canonical litmus test by reference. The refusal copy must explain how to either reframe the item as a durable SC or move it to Closure Checks."
4. **Approval gate output well-formed** — :30: "`phases/approval-gate.md` shows that the approved epic spec retains the SC / Closure Checks separation (it does not silently merge them at approval time)."
**Out of scope (:34-39):** general brainstorm dialogue quality; Phase 1 Socratic probe choice; UX probe design; cross-epic interaction scan logic; anything other than the SC-vs-Closure-Checks distinction.
**workflow_stage:** `"brainstorm"`. **scoring:** PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL per shared rubric.

### 2.2 — Chunk B: preplanning — chunk-b-preplanning.md
**Files to read:** `:11-14` — preplanning SKILL.md; `preplanning/prompts/*`; `preplanning/docs/reviewers/*` (if present); any other file under `skills/preplanning/` referenced from SKILL.md.
**Validation property (:18 "if and only if ALL of the following hold"):**
1. **SC inheritance is partitioned** — :20: decomposition "does not silently fold Closure Check items into story DDs. Either: (a) Closure Checks remain on the epic and stories carry only end-state DDs, OR (b) the SKILL.md explicitly names a story-level Closure Checks partition policy."
2. **End-state-only DD semantics** — :22: "Story Done Definitions inherit the end-state-only character of their parent Success Criteria. The preplanning prompts must not generate transitional DDs (e.g., \"OAuth migration is complete\") for stories whose parent SC is end-state (\"the system supports OAuth\")."
3. **Distinction preserved through decomposition** — :24: prompt files loaded via SKILL.md "must not collapse the SC / Closure Checks distinction. The Closure Checks remain visible to downstream consumers (implementation-plan, completion-verifier) after preplanning completes."
4. **Annotation discipline** — :26: "If preplanning emits \"Satisfies <SC-id>\" or similar annotations on draft stories, those annotations must distinguish SC-satisfaction from Closure-Check-satisfaction."
**Out of scope (:30-35):** general story-decomposition quality; adversarial review machinery (red/blue team); UI designer dispatch logic; scrutiny pipeline reuse from brainstorm; anything else.
**workflow_stage:** `"preplanning"`.

### 2.3 — Chunk C: implementation-plan — chunk-c-implementation-plan.md
**Files to read:** `:11-14` — implementation-plan SKILL.md; `implementation-plan/prompts/*`; `implementation-plan/docs/reviewers/*` (if present); any other referenced file under the skill.
**Validation property (:18 "if and only if ALL of the following hold"):**
1. **Task AC partitioning excludes Closure Checks** — :20: "Closure Check items from the parent epic MUST NOT be assigned to tasks as task-level Acceptance Criteria. Closure Checks are validated once at epic closure by the completion-verifier, not per-task."
2. **AC text does not conflate** — :22: "Task Acceptance Criteria templates and the AC library do not mix end-state and transitional phrasing. Tasks may produce one-time outcomes (their work is by definition transitional), but the AC text must not claim to verify a Closure Check."
3. **No reverse propagation** — :24: "The implementation-plan workflow does not write Closure Check items back into the epic or into story DDs as a side-effect of task decomposition. Closure Checks are a property of the epic; tasks do not author them."
4. **AC library distinguishes if it references Closure Checks at all** — :26: "If `docs/ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA-LIBRARY.md` (or its equivalent) mentions Closure Checks, the mention must distinguish AC verification from Closure Check verification."
**Out of scope (:30-35):** general task-decomposition quality; approach proposal generation; file-impact enrichment; retry budget design; anything else.
**workflow_stage:** `"implementation-plan"`.

### 2.4 — Chunk D: sprint — chunk-d-sprint.md (6 sub-clauses — the richest conjunction)
**Files to read:** `:11-14` — sprint SKILL.md; `sprint/prompts/*`; `agents/completion-verifier.md`; "Any other file referenced from sprint SKILL.md's Phase F Step 18 (story closure) or Phase G Step 2 (epic closure) verifier-dispatch path".
**Validation property (:18 "if and only if ALL of the following hold"):**
1. **Verifier reads Closure Checks separately** — :20: "`agents/completion-verifier.md` has code paths that read the `## Closure Checks` section distinctly from end-state items (Success Criteria for epics, Done Definitions for stories). The verifier validates each Closure Check one-shot at closure."
2. **Subtree walk via parent_id only** — :22: "The completion-verifier walks descendant tickets via `parent_id` only — NOT `relates_to` or `depends_on`. This avoids shared-descendant fan-out blocking. If the agent file walks via any other link type, that is a critical finding."
3. **Closed/deleted descendants skipped** — :24: "The verifier skips descendants in `closed` or `deleted` status during the subtree walk. Blocked status is treated as open unless all blockers are terminal."
4. **Legacy compatibility** — :26: "Tickets without a `## Closure Checks` section are treated as having zero Closure Checks (a pass), preserving legacy compatibility. Absence of the section is not a failure mode."
5. **Sprint orchestrator dispatches the verifier with the correct ticket scope** — :28: "Phase F Step 18 dispatches the verifier with a story ID; Phase G Step 2 dispatches with an epic ID. The dispatch shape (named subagent_type, fallback to general-purpose with verbatim agent file) is not inline-paraphrased."
6. **Verifier emits WARN, not BLOCK, on open descendants without Closure Checks** — :30: "The completion-verifier output format distinguishes \"open descendant has unresolved Closure Check\" (BLOCK) from \"open descendant exists but has no Closure Checks at all\" (WARN)."
**Out of scope (:34-39):** general sprint orchestration design; Phase 1 heuristic coherence-walk.sh ("it is Phase 1; you are Phase 2"); batch dispatch and worktree isolation; sub-agent boundary rules; anything else.
**workflow_stage:** `"sprint"`.
**overlap:** Sub-clauses 2/3/6 mirror completion-verifier (catalog 5.1, completion-verifier.md:160-203) — parent_id-only walk, skip closed/deleted, BLOCK-vs-WARN. This chunk is the coherence-walk *validation* of that gate.

### 2.5 — Chunk E: commit-and-review — chunk-e-commit-and-review.md
**Files to read:** `:11-20` — docs/workflows/COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md, REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md; agents/code-reviewer-light.md, -standard.md, -deep-arch.md, -deep-correctness.md, -deep-hygiene.md, -deep-verification.md, -arbiter.md; "Any other `agents/code-reviewer-*.md` file present in the repo".
**Validation property (:24 "if and only if ALL of the following hold"):**
1. **Reviewers do not author Closure Checks** — :26: "Code review findings are limited to code (diff, files modified, tests, lint, security). Reviewers do not add or remove Closure Check items on tickets. The review-workflow prompts must not direct reviewers to evaluate Closure Checks."
2. **Review verdict does not block Closure Check verification** — :28: "REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and the code-reviewer agents emit findings on code, not on planning artifacts. A review PASS does not imply Closure Checks pass; the completion-verifier is the separate authority for that. If review and verifier are conflated in any file, that is a finding."
3. **Failure surfaces remain distinct** — :30: "A code review FAIL and a Closure Check FAIL produce visibly different signals in the workflow (different artifacts, different blocking-gate identifiers, different remediation paths). Reviewers do not silently absorb Closure Check failures into their findings, and the completion-verifier does not silently absorb code findings."
4. **Commit workflow does not mutate Closure Checks** — :32: "COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md does not edit ticket descriptions, add Closure Check items, or transition Closure Check state. The commit phase records artifacts (test status, review status, reviewer findings hash) without touching planning intent."
**Out of scope (:36-41):** general code-review tier classification quality; reviewer model selection (light/standard/deep); autonomous resolution loop design; false-positive escape valve (`/dso:fp-recovery`); anything else.
**workflow_stage:** `"commit-and-review"`.

### 2.6 — Chunk F: operational-dry-run — chunk-f-operational-dry-run.md (structurally different)
**Structural note (:7):** "This chunk is structurally different from chunks A–E. The others read static workflow files. You read the **output of a preview script** that the orchestrator ran before dispatching you." Input is JSON from `scripts/closure-checks-migration-preview.sh`, appended under "### Preview script output" (placeholder `<<<PREVIEW_OUTPUT_PLACEHOLDER>>>` at :64). Per-item JSON shape (:13-25): `epic_id, epic_title, items_total, items_classified_end_state, items_classified_transitional, items_classified_uncertain, sample_transitional_items[]`.
**Preview-failure path (:27):** if the script failed (no API key / no `brainstorm:complete`-tagged closed epics / script error), orchestrator passes empty array `[]` + a `preview_error` field → reviewer must emit `status: "AMBIGUOUS"` with a finding naming the preview-script failure.
**Validation property (:31 "The migration prevents future false-positive closures if and only if"):**
1. **Classifier yield** — :33: "Across the 10 sampled epics, the classifier identifies at least one item per epic on average that would be re-classified as transitional or uncertain." If zero across 10 epics: either population already clean (PASS, flag low yield as informational) OR classifier mis-tuned (FAIL).
2. **Sample plausibility / mis-classification spot-check** — :35: "The transitional / uncertain items, sampled in `sample_transitional_items`, are plausibly transitional. Spot-check 2–3 sample items per epic. If they are clearly end-state items being mis-classified as transitional (\"the system supports OAuth\" → transitional is wrong), that is a FAIL with a finding naming the classifier prompt as the resolution path."
3. **Uncertain-rate ceiling** — :37: "No epic has `items_classified_uncertain` ≥ 50% of `items_total`. A high uncertain rate means the classifier prompt is under-specified... >= 50% uncertain in any epic is an important finding."
**Verdict mapping (chunk-local, :39-43):**
- :41 — "All three properties hold → `status: \"PASS\"`. Cite the per-epic counts in `evidence_citations` using `epic_id` as the file field and `0` as the line field (since this is data, not a file)."
- :42 — "Property 1 fails on the \"classifier mis-tuned\" interpretation OR property 2 finds clear mis-classifications → `status: \"FAIL\"` with `severity: \"critical\"` or `\"important\"` findings."
- :43 — "Property 3 fails OR sample items are ambiguous OR preview script returned `preview_error` → `status: \"AMBIGUOUS\"`."
**Out of scope (:45-51):** classifier prompt design (story ad70's scope); migration script correctness (Phase 4 cutover); API cost; anything other than "do the counts and samples support the claim that the migration prevents false-positive closures?"
**workflow_stage:** `"operational-dry-run"`.

---

## SECTION 3 — Plan-review 4-dimension rubric

File: `docs/workflows/prompts/plan-review-dispatch.md` (57 lines). The shared prompt template filled by both the SKILL (Step 1) and the agent. Substitutes `{artifact_type}` and `{artifact content}` placeholders.

**Framing line** — plan-review-dispatch.md:1-2:
> ":1 — "You are reviewing a {artifact_type} before it is presented to the user for approval."
> :2 — "Your job is to find real problems — not to nitpick or add unnecessary suggestions."

**Scoring scale** — :10: "Score each dimension 1-5 (5 = no issues found)".

### 3.1 — Feasibility — :12-16
**what:** "Can this actually be built as described?"
Sub-checks (literal): ":14 — Are there missing steps or impossible constraints?"; ":15 — Do the proposed tools/libraries/APIs exist and work as assumed?"; ":16 — Are there implicit dependencies that aren't called out?"
**scoring:** 1-5.

### 3.2 — Completeness — :18-22
**what:** "Does the plan cover what it needs to?"
Sub-checks: ":20 — Are error cases and edge cases addressed where they matter?"; ":21 — Is the testing strategy adequate?"; ":22 — Are integration points with existing code identified?"
**scoring:** 1-5.

### 3.3 — YAGNI / Overengineering — :24-29 (includes config-surface sub-check)
**what:** "Is the plan doing too much?"
Sub-checks: ":26 — Are there unnecessary abstractions or premature generalizations?"; ":27 — Could anything be simplified without losing value?"; ":28 — Are there features or capabilities that weren't asked for?"
**Config-surface check (bug 70fe-1c39)** — :29, literal:
> "**Config-surface check** (bug 70fe-1c39): does the plan introduce a new `dso-config.conf` key? If so, has the plan surveyed existing keys in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/config/dso-config.reference.conf` and explained why none can be extended or reinterpreted? Flag plans that mint new keys without an explicit consolidation rationale — config proliferation produces contradictory derived modes and silent misconfigurations."
**scoring:** 1-5.
**overlap:** Same concept as the approach-decision-maker anti-pattern "config-surface proliferation" (catalog 3.4, approach-decision-maker.md:115-127) and complexity-gate Gate1 YAGNI (catalog 3.9).

### 3.4 — Codebase Alignment — :31-35
**what:** "Does the plan match how this project actually works?"
Sub-checks: ":33 — Does it follow existing naming conventions and file organization?"; ":34 — Does it use the project's established patterns (not invent new ones)?"; ":35 — Are the referenced files, modules, and APIs accurate?"
**scoring:** 1-5.
**overlap:** Mirrors approach-decision-maker "Codebase Alignment" dim (catalog 3.4) and project-alignment reviewer (catalog 3.1).

### 3.5 — Output format contract — :38-56
- ":41 — VERDICT: PASS or REVISE"
- ":43-47 — SCORES: feasibility / completeness / yagni / codebase_alignment, each `N/5`"
- ":49-56 — FINDINGS: \"For any dimension scoring below 4, list specific issues\"; per-finding shape `FINDING: [dimension] [severity: critical|major|minor]` + description + `SUGGESTION:` (how to fix).
**severity vocabulary:** {critical, major, minor} — note **major** (vs coherence-walk's **important**).

---

## SECTION 4 — Plan-review SKILL gating + agent + hook

### 4.1 — `skills/plan-review/SKILL.md` (118 lines)

**Sub-agent guard** — SKILL.md:7-9, literal:
> "Requires Agent tool. If running as a sub-agent (Agent tool unavailable), STOP and return: \"ERROR: /dso:plan-review requires Agent tool; invoke from orchestrator.\"" — orchestrator-level-only enforcement.

**When to invoke (MANDATORY)** — SKILL.md:17-23: presenting a design doc for approval (brainstorming skill); presenting an implementation plan (writing-plans skill); calling `ExitPlanMode`. ":22 — The hook on `ExitPlanMode` enforces this mechanically. For brainstorming and writing-plans, this is skill-enforced."

**Parameters** — SKILL.md:26-30: `artifact_type` (required; `"design"` or `"implementation_plan"`); `artifact_path` (required; path or inline content).

**artifact_type → model branch** — SKILL.md:37-54:
> ":39 — `artifact_type: "design"` → `opus` (architectural reasoning)
> :40 — `artifact_type: "implementation_plan"` → `sonnet` (structural analysis)"
> Model rationale (:50-53 comment): "design needs trade-off reasoning across architectural coherence (opus); implementation_plan needs structured analysis of task atomicity / dependency graph / TDD discipline (sonnet — haiku misses implicit coupling)."
**Dispatch shape** — :43: "**Inline dispatch is required — `dso:plan-review` is an agent file identifier, NOT a valid `subagent_type` value.**" Read `agents/plan-review.md` inline, dispatch as `subagent_type: "general-purpose"` with the model above (:45-54). (NOTE: this contradicts the agent file's own frontmatter — see Section 5 NEW finding.)

**Schema validation pre-gate** — SKILL.md:58-70:
> ":58 — "First, validate the sub-agent's output schema (schema-hash: 9dba6875b85b7bc3)"
> Runs `".claude/scripts/dso validate-review-output.sh" plan-review "$PLAN_OUT"` (:67), writing output to `$(get_artifacts_dir)/plan-review-output.txt`.
> :70 — "If `SCHEMA_VALID: no` — send a correction prompt to the sub-agent requesting the exact format; do not proceed until validation passes." (loop-until-valid)

**PASS / REVISE thresholds** — SKILL.md:74,79:
- :74 — "**If VERDICT is PASS** (all scores >= 4)" → write marker, present plan, note "This plan was reviewed by a sub-agent. No issues found." (:75-77)
- :79 — "**If VERDICT is REVISE** (any score < 4)" → remediate.

**Remediation ordering (one pass)** — SKILL.md:80-86, literal:
> ":80 — "Address each finding by severity: critical first, then major, then minor"
> :81 — "For critical/major findings: revise the plan directly"
> :82 — "For minor findings: revise if quick, otherwise note them for the user"
> :83 — "**Do NOT re-run the review** — one pass is enough. Note what was changed.""
Then write marker + present revised plan with summary (:84-86).

**Marker file contract** — SKILL.md:88-106: written after pass-or-revise to `$(get_artifacts_dir)/plan-review-status` via `get_artifacts_dir` from `hooks/lib/deps.sh`. Contents (:97-103):
```
passed
timestamp=<ISO8601 UTC>
artifact_type={design|implementation_plan}
verdict={PASS|REVISE}
scores=feasibility:{N},completeness:{N},yagni:{N},codebase_alignment:{N}
```
":106 — "The `ExitPlanMode` hook checks for this file." (first line `passed` is the gate token.)

**Explicit non-features** — SKILL.md:108-114: "No multi-stage review protocol (single pass only)"; "No JSON schema output (findings are for the agent, not persisted)"; "No automated revision cycles (agent fixes once, done)"; "No conflict detection between findings". (Contrasts coherence-walk's JSON-object contract.)

**Wrong-tool guard** — SKILL.md:115-117: "Common Mistake: Do NOT Use Code Review for Plans" — `/dso:review` is for completed code (diffs/coverage/bugs); use `/dso:plan-review` for plans. References CLAUDE.md `always:correct-review-tool`.

### 4.2 — `agents/plan-review.md` (20 lines)
**Frontmatter** — :1-6: `name: plan-review`; **`model: sonnet`** (static); description says "Use subagent_type \"dso:plan-review\" to dispatch this agent." (:4); `color: red`.
**Body** — :8-12: "You are a plan and design reviewer... You apply the review rubric from `docs/workflows/prompts/plan-review-dispatch.md`." Reads + executes the dispatch template, substituting `{artifact_type}` and `{artifact content}`.
**Constraints** — :14-19, literal: "Do NOT modify any files — this is review only."; "Do NOT dispatch sub-agents or Task calls."; "Do NOT stage or commit changes."; "Return your output conforming to the schema in `plan-review-dispatch.md`."

### 4.3 — plan-review-gate.sh hook
**NOT readable in this task's file set** — the SKILL references the `ExitPlanMode` hook (:22, :106) but the hook script itself is not among the four files I was scoped to read. The catalog's description of it (4.4) is therefore unverified by this pass; see reconciliation NOT-FOUND note below.

---

## SECTION 5 — RECONCILIATION against catalog Part 4 (lines 143-174)

Format: catalog claim → CONFIRMED (citation) / NOT-FOUND (flag).

### Part 4.1 — coherence-walk shared rubric (catalog :146-153)

## SECTION 6 — NEW (in files, absent or under-captured in catalog Part 4)

These were present in the read files but are **not** itemized in catalog Part 4:

1. **The universal narrow question + "What you are NOT answering" exclusion list** — verdict-rubric.md:7-16. The catalog folds this into "single-question scoping" but does not quote the shared question itself ("does this workflow correctly handle the SC-vs-Closure-Checks distinction now that the epic has shipped its template, validator hook, and verifier refactor?", :9) nor the 5-item NOT-answering list (:13). Load-bearing for the anti-FP scoping design.

2. **JSON-only output contract + required-field list** — verdict-rubric.md:55-59. Catalog Part 4 doesn't note the output shape (`workflow_stage`, `status`, `evidence_citations`, `findings`; optional `tech_failure`) or the "JSON object alone, no markdown fence" rule. This is the machine-contract counterpart to plan-review's structured-text format — a real cross-surface divergence (JSON vs `VERDICT:/SCORES:/FINDINGS:` text).

3. **`tech_failure` field — orchestrator-only** — verdict-rubric.md:57: "always omit or set to `false` — only the orchestrator sets `tech_failure: true`." A distinct outcome channel separate from the 3-valued verdict (tech failure ≠ FAIL). Not in catalog.

4. **Three worked examples encoding the verdict/severity/citation contract** — verdict-rubric.md:69-130 (PASS :71-86, important→FAIL :88-108, AMBIGUOUS :110-129). Notably Example 2 is the **completion-verifier relates_to-walk → critical FAIL** case (:90-104) — the same defect Chunk D sub-clause 2 polices; the rubric ships its own canonical FAIL exemplar.

5. **Severity-vocabulary divergence between the two surfaces** — coherence-walk uses **critical / important / minor** (verdict-rubric.md:51-53); plan-review uses **critical / major / minor** (plan-review-dispatch.md:53 + SKILL.md:80). The middle tier differs by name (important vs major). Worth flagging for our gate so we pick one vocabulary.

6. **Chunk F's chunk-local verdict mapping overrides/specializes the shared rubric** — chunk-f-operational-dry-run.md:39-43. Unlike A–E (which defer wholly to the shared rubric), F supplies its own PASS/FAIL/AMBIGUOUS mapping AND a data-citation convention (`epic_id` as file field, `0` as line field, :41). Also the `preview_error` → forced-AMBIGUOUS path (:27, :43). Catalog 4.2 lists F's three properties but not its bespoke mapping/citation convention.

7. **SUB-AGENT-GUARD on plan-review SKILL** — SKILL.md:7-9. The orchestrator-only enforcement ("ERROR: /dso:plan-review requires Agent tool; invoke from orchestrator") is a gating mechanism not captured in catalog 4.4.

8. **Schema-correction retry loop** — SKILL.md:70: "do not proceed until validation passes" — a loop-until-valid behavior distinct from the "one pass is enough" remediation rule (:83). Catalog notes "schema validation pre-gate" but not that schema-invalidity triggers a correction *re-prompt* loop (whereas content REVISE is single-pass).

9. **Model-dispatch contradiction (agent frontmatter vs SKILL)** — `agents/plan-review.md:2` hard-codes `model: sonnet` and `:4` says dispatch via `subagent_type: "dso:plan-review"`, but `SKILL.md:43` declares `dso:plan-review` is "NOT a valid `subagent_type` value" and instructs inline dispatch as `general-purpose` with model branched **opus(design)/sonnet(impl-plan)**. The agent file's static `sonnet` is overridden for designs. An internal inconsistency worth noting if we model our gate on this precedent.

10. **plan-review explicit non-features list** — SKILL.md:108-114 (no multi-stage protocol, no JSON output, no automated revision cycles, no conflict detection). Directly contrasts coherence-walk (which IS multi-chunk, JSON-output). Relevant design-axis catalog Part 4 doesn't surface.

---

## KEY TAKEAWAYS (3-valued verdict + anti-FP disciplines — emphasized per request)

- The **3-valued PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL** system lives entirely in `verdict-rubric.md:19-48`, shared verbatim across all six chunks. AMBIGUOUS is explicitly the *de-escalation target*: "prefer AMBIGUOUS with a specific question over FAIL with a hand-waved claim" (:63). This is the lever that maps to our block(FAIL)/advise(AMBIGUOUS)/clear(PASS) split.
- The **anti-FP disciplines are three named, literal-quoted sections** — single-question scoping (:7-16), cite-or-omit (:61-63), refuse-to-invent/no-speculation (:65-67) — plus the severity-to-runtime-impact tie (:51-53). The cite-or-omit rationale is explicit about *why*: "findings without evidence are change-detector noise — they create false positives that block legitimate migrations." These are the design's stated mechanism for reliable low-FP Sonnet review.
- **plan-review is the closest existing GATE precedent** (binary PASS/REVISE, marker-file→ExitPlanMode hook, severity-ordered single-pass remediation) but uses a different (2-valued) verdict and a different middle-severity word (major vs important). The catalog's note that plan-review-gate.sh "fails OPEN / no force-flag / fires on ExitPlanMode" could not be verified from the four scoped files (the hook script is outside this read set) — recommend reading `hooks/` to confirm before mirroring it.

All 23 Part-4 catalog sub-items are confirmed citation-accurate against the source (3 trivial line-range truncations noted); 1 item (the hook script) is out-of-scope-NOT-FOUND; 10 NEW items surfaced.
